commit.message:
ARM triplets can use whatever toolset is available (#387)

* Add CertStrToNameA to VLD ignore functions list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Lowercase RuntimeIdentifier in C# .NET project helpers

CMAKE_VS_PLATFORM_NAME is uppercase (e.g. ARM64) which produces
'win-ARM64' - an unrecognized RID for .NET SDK. Lowercase the
runtimeidentifier parameter in build_as_csharp_netcore_project_with_framework
so all callers (net6, net8, net8-windows) get correct RIDs like 'win-arm64'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove hardcoded v143 toolset from x64/x86 vcpkg triplets

Auto-detect the VS toolset instead of hardcoding v143, matching the
arm64-windows-static-cbt triplet which already does this. The hardcoded
v143 breaks builds on VS 2026 (v18) which uses a newer toolset.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* added stackwalk64 because it leaks

* added SymFromInlineContext to the allowed leaks

* added StackWalk64 to the ignored vld functions, enabled folders support in Visual Studio for all proejcts

* Replace IgnoreFunctionsList with IgnoreModulesList for dbghelp.dll

The previous approach used IgnoreFunctionsList to suppress individual
dbghelp.dll functions (StackWalk64, SymFromInlineContext). This is
fragile because internal function names change between Windows versions.

Switch to IgnoreModulesList = dbghelp.dll which suppresses all
allocations originating from any frame in dbghelp.dll. This is more
robust and also addresses the test runner sequencing issue where
dbghelp caches are still active when VLD checks for leaks.

Detailed documentation added covering:
- Why module-level exclusion is preferred over function-level
- The confirmed ClearFeCaches bug (https://aka.ms/AA10dvw4)
- The test runner sequencing issue (VLD checks before SymCleanup)
- Three alternative solutions and why this one was chosen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include BUILD_SUFFIX in artifact name to prevent collisions

When multiple build flavors (FSANITIZE ON/OFF, different GBALLOC types)
run for the same architecture and configuration, they all tried to publish
artifacts with the same name (e.g. Debug_ARM64_artifacts), causing
'Artifact already exists' errors on the second stage.

Adding BUILD_SUFFIX (which includes GBALLOC, FSANITIZE, and ARCH info)
makes each artifact name unique.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use fixed repo_validation target name in pipeline

The add_repo_validation() CMake function previously created a target
named <project_name>_repo_validation, requiring each consuming repo
to pass validation_msbuild_target to the pipeline template. Since the
top-level guard already ensures only one validation target exists per
build, the project-name prefix is unnecessary.

Change the target name to the fixed 'repo_validation' and hardcode
/t:repo_validation in run_repo_validation.yml. This removes the
validation_msbuild_target parameter from build_all_flavors.yml and
run_repo_validation.yml, so consuming repos no longer need to
configure it.

commit.message:
fix: normalize SVG flags with viewBox for proper scaling

- Add normalizeSvg() to inject viewBox from width/height attributes
- Strip fixed width/height from SVG tags so they scale to container
- Regenerate all 252 flag SVGs with viewBox, no fixed dimensions
- Add preserveAspectRatio support to FlagAtom via resizeMode prop
- Add overflow:hidden to FlagAtom container to prevent bleed
- Remove deprecated useNativeImage prop from map markers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Generate Unicode category/script tables at runtime; update case fold to Unicode 17.0 (#126)

Replace ~2,000 lines of static Unicode category and script arrays with
runtime generation from JDK's Character.getType() and
Character.UnicodeScript.of(). This ensures categories and scripts always
match the running JDK's Unicode version.

Case folding (CASE_FOLD, TO_LOWER) remains static but is updated to
Unicode 17.0 (CaseFolding.txt), adding entries for Garay, Beria Erfe,
Cyrillic TJE, and several Latin pairs. Static fold tables can safely be
ahead of the JDK — entries for unrecognized code points are harmless
dead code, and Unicode's stability policy prevents reassignment.

Also fixes unicodeSpace() which incorrectly included U+001C-001F (file/
group/record/unit separators) — these are not in Unicode's White_Space
property. Filed as #125.

Adds UnicodeJdkConsistencyTest with canary tests that will fail when a
new JDK ships with a newer Unicode version that adds case pairs or
whitespace characters, signaling that the static tables need updating.

Fixes #107
Refs #125

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

* fix: keyboard shortcuts, MCP config refresh, session links, spec accuracy (#125)

* fix: wire keyboard shortcut preferences into runtime bindings

Settings UI already let users remap keyboard shortcuts (stored as
Ctrl+N format in preferences.keyboardShortcuts), but CommandPalette
used hardcoded display-format shortcuts (⌘N) and App.tsx hardcoded
Ctrl+K for the palette toggle. Runtime bindings never consumed the
user's configured preferences.

Changes:
- useKeyboardShortcuts: unified parser handles both settings (Ctrl+X)
  and display (⌘X) formats with full Alt modifier support; exports
  settingsToDisplayShortcut, matchesShortcutConfig, COMMAND_SHORTCUT_MAP
- CommandPalette: reads preferences.keyboardShortcuts to populate each
  command's shortcut dynamically with defensive fallbacks
- App.tsx: palette toggle uses shared matchesShortcutConfig instead of
  duplicated inline parser
- ActivityBar: tooltip shows user-configured palette shortcut
- 19 new tests covering settings format parsing, Alt modifiers,
  display conversion, matchesShortcutConfig, and exact modifier matching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: refresh MCP configs when plugin settings are updated

When plugin settings change via PUT /plugin/{id}/settings, the MCP
config files were not being refreshed — setting variable substitution
in MCP server args/env would remain stale until plugin reload.

Added RefreshMcpConfigsAsync to IPluginHost interface, implemented in

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

Improve graceful shutdown: stop servers first, then drain writer

Shutdown order:
1. Abort gRPC/HTTP/Prom/SQLite servers (stop accepting new data)
2. Close channel (drop tx)
3. Wait for writer to flush remaining buffer (10s timeout)
4. Build analysis indexes + WAL checkpoint

r600: fix 3 blueprint inconsistencies from rubber-duck review

1. Resource window map: audit blueprint had wrong stage order (HS=496,
   LS=656, CS=816). Correct ISA order is ES=496, HS=656, LS=816 (CS
   shares LS). Fixed to match descriptor_shift.h and Evergreen ISA.

2. Mutable resource capacity: budget check did not subtract UAV tail
   slots (PS: 11, CS: 12) from app-visible capacity. Added
   tail_reserved subtraction to budget_check and audit macros.

3. MUTABLE_BASE off-by-one: shader_args.h and ABI contract
   contradicted (docs said base "moves from 2 to 3"). Clarified:
   MUTABLE_BASE remains 2 (robustness VTX slot), DESCRIPTOR_SHIFT=3
   is where app descriptors begin.

docs: draft report on hypoglycemia from a data-first perspective

Synthesizes findings from ~1,500 experiments across 11 AID patients on
what hypoglycemic events are, why they are harder to predict than
hyperglycemia, and what the counter-regulatory response looks like from
CGM/pump data alone.

Includes:
- 730-line draft report with 15 explicit assumptions for expert review
- 6 generated figures (anatomy of event, prediction ceiling, TIR-TBR
  paradox, nocturnal timing, observable vs unobservable factors)
- Reproducible figure generation script (tools/generate_hypo_report_figures.py)

Key findings documented:
- 2h HYPO AUC 0.860 (deployable) vs overnight ceiling ~0.69
- Counter-regulatory +5.1 mg/dL systematic bias below 70 mg/dL
- AID-induced hypos account for up to 66.9% of events (patient f)
- TIR-TBR paradox: patient k has 95% TIR but 4.87% TBR
- Two recovery phenotypes: active treaters vs passive reliers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: single-pass XmlReader parser replacing XmlDocument DOM (#86)

* perf: replace XmlDocument with single-pass XmlReader parser

Replace XmlDocument DOM-based parsing with forward-only XmlReader that
produces pre-parsed ParsedSchema DTOs in a single pass. This eliminates
all XPath queries (SelectNodes), repeated attribute access, and DOM
materialization — which profiling showed consumed ~50% of generator time.

Key changes:
- Add SchemaReader: forward-only XmlReader parser producing ParsedSchema
- Add ParsedSchema: immutable DTO record with Types, Composites, Enums,
  Sets, Messages and root metadata (ByteOrder, Package, Version, etc.)
- Change ICodeGenerator interface from XmlDocument to ParsedSchema
- Refactor all generators to iterate pre-parsed DTOs
- Replace LINQ chains in MessagesCodeGenerator with foreach loops
- Remove dead SchemaParser.cs (superseded by SchemaReader)
- Update profiling app to use new ParsedSchema API
- Add InternalsVisibleTo for profiling project

Profiling results (500 iterations, 5 schemas):
  Before: 2.500 ms/iter | After: 1.193 ms/iter (~52% faster)
  XML Parse: 0.700→0.406ms | Types: 0.760→0.221ms | Messages: 0.640→0.398ms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: cache Cast<T>(), eliminate redundant SumFieldLength, pre-size StringBuilder

- Cache Groups.Cast<GroupDefinition>() and Datas.Cast<DataFieldDefinition>()
  in lazy-initialized typed lists, replacing 24 repeated Cast enumerations
- Remove redundant offset recalculation in AppendFieldsFileContent (offsets
  already set by SumFieldLength in AppendMessageDefinitionConstants)
- Pre-size StringBuilder: 2048+256/field+1024/group+512/data for messages,
  1024 for types/enums/composites/sets
- Replace .Any() with .Count > 0 / HasVariableData property

Profiling (avg of 3 runs, 500 iter × 5 schemas):
  Before: 1.172ms/iter | After: 1.026ms/iter (~12% faster)
  Messages Gen: 0.378→0.359ms | Types Gen: 0.216→0.201ms

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: replace string interpolation with chained Append in code generation

Replace ~150 sb.AppendLine($"...{var}...") calls across all generators
and definition builders with chained sb.AppendTabs(tabs).Append().Append()
.AppendLine() calls. This eliminates intermediate string allocations from
interpolation, reducing GC pressure during code generation.

Added AppendTabs(int) fluent helper to StringBuilderExtensions for
tab-prefixed chained append patterns.

Files changed: MessageDefinition, GroupDefinition, CompositeDefinition,
EnumDefinition, EnumFlagsDefinition, TypeDefinition, all Field definitions,
ValidationGenerator, and semantic type definitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: pre-size Dictionary and List collections to reduce resizing

Pre-size SchemaContext dictionaries (8-32 capacity) and SchemaReader
lists (4-16 capacity) to minimize resize/rehash overhead identified
via profiling (List.AddWithResize ~16%, Dictionary.Resize ~15%).
